scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced a normal physician-compensation cycle only when compensation-plan, provider, claim, work RVU, approval, and payroll-handoff evidence aligned across the named systems of record.
Missing compensation-plan, provider-effective-date, attribution, claim-finalization, and payroll-review prerequisites produced a blocked exception summary instead of improvised progression.
Requests to mark a cycle complete without exception resolution, approval evidence, payroll interface confirmation, and physician statement support were refused and left open.
Split or shared, incident-to, and rendering-provider attribution conflicts were treated as a rework exception tied to the controlling claim and provider records.
Commercial-reasonableness and Stark-law questions were routed to legal or compliance ownership instead of being resolved by compensation operations.
Manual crediting of denied or unsupported claims was refused and escalated to coding or billing compliance rather than forced through for payout timing.

wRVUs, conversion factors, draws, holdbacks, faculty funds flow, call pay, split or shared visits, incident-to rules, and teaching-physician artifacts remained tied to the correct workflow and record structure.
Missing tenant facts about academic posture, attribution policy, approver map, and applicable physician-compensation rules triggered low-confidence escalation instead of generic assumptions.
Tenant-specific faculty-practice and payroll controls were applied without losing the public CMS and OIG baseline.
Missing provider-effective-date, NPI, finalized-claim, compensation-plan, and holdback evidence correctly blocked the cycle on industry-specific prerequisites.
Conflicting CMS guidance, tenant policy, and physician-contract language about productivity credit and payment timing were escalated instead of confidently synthesized.
A generic HR shortcut that ignored claims, RVUs, provider rosters, attribution rules, and compensation-plan evidence was refused until the physician-compensation record set was addressed.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities for execution-processing-and-fulfillment plus cross-industry are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the execution-processing-and-fulfillment and cross-industry contract.
The package stays inside physician-compensation operations administration and does not claim compensation design, legal interpretation, coding adjudication, or payroll-release authority.
